response: {"purpose":"Figure 2d examines the reproducibility of SLBP eCLIP signal across two biological replicates by plotting the SMInput-normalized fold-enrichment of clusters identified in Replicate 1 against the corresponding fold-enrichment measured in Replicate 2. By colour-coding histone-overlapping clusters (pink) and significantly enriched peaks (blue), the panel illustrates that biologically meaningful binding sites — particularly those on histone RNAs, the known targets of SLBP — show concordant enrichment in both replicates, whereas non-specific clusters do not. The attached marginal histograms further summarise the distribution of significantly enriched peaks in each replicate independently, providing a visual assessment of signal consistency. This cross-replicate comparison matters because it demonstrates that SMInput normalization retains and highlights true SLBP binding events while reducing noise, thereby validating the approach as a reliable method for identifying reproducible protein–RNA interactions from eCLIP data.","title":"SMInput Normalization Improves SLBP eCLIP Replicate Reproducibility"}
